description:
In Scope

In this feature, we will cover the Build & Test step components.

  • Build Section (the section is collapsible)
    • Environment actions bar
      • Showing the env of the launched TPK
      • Showing the status and the different actions (same as the one done in the upgrade)
      • Add to it the "Open Config Editor" button (this is only available in the CI process)
      • Plus, to the right, we are adding a TPK repush button (repush a TPK) & a report icon to open the TPK details page so that users can see the test report
    • "You are working on the following stories" + link to the Jira stories
    • "Commits on branch name" table: listing the commits on the dev branch
      • Support empty state & full
      • Show the following details:
        • Commit ID: short format
        • Description
        • User
        • Commit Date (following the date format that we are using everywhere in the platform)
  • Test Section (the section is collapsible)
    • Environment action bar
      • Showing the env of the launched TPK
      • Showing the status and the different actions (same as the one done in the upgrade)
      • Add to it the "Config Audit" button
        • It is a dropdown giving access to the HTML and CSV reports
        • Color coding and icons are added to this button
          • red for failures
          • green for passed
          • blue for still running
      • NO TPK repush or TPK report buttons --> no need since the TPK card is already available in this section
    • Select TPK to run
    • TPK Results
      • Support empty state when no TPk is yet executed
      • Show the TPK card component for the latest TPK run --> this component works normally as an aggregate (test unit)
      • "Show Previous Runs" table: to display previous runs, whether coming from one TPk definition or multiple
  • Merge button: this will open the MR popup, which includes, in this case, the backport inputs

 

NB: please keep all confirmation popups & tooltips:

  • When repushing or running a new TPK --> confirmation pop-up to keep services up & previous runs will be cleaned
  • When a TPk is running, hovering over the TPK run button and the repush will show a tooltip explaining why the buttons are disabled, and users can't push another one
  • etc...

 

Technical Reseed & On-demand Backport

Make sure to cover the 2 special BP base definitions in the CI process:

  • Technical Reseed: additional section for it in the Build &Test step
    • https://docs.murex.com/mxevolve-studio/pages/viewpage?pageId=554476048
  • On-Demand Backport: additional banners telling the user what's happening in the background and what's coming next
    • https://docs.murex.com/mxevolve-studio/pages/viewpage?pageId=619111518

Please rely on the documentation and Krys's mockup for more details.
